channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar will run for reelection in the U.S. House, not an open Minnesota Senate seat
item.description: U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar had been considering a run for the seat currently held by Sen. Tina Smith. The DFLer will instead seek a fifth House term.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Fact check: Trump's tariff, international trade claims
item.description: NewsNation fact-checked some of Trump's claims about tariffs and U.S. trade relationships to separate hyperbole from fact.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Where did U.S. humanities grants go? To projects from a baseball film to AI research
item.description: From AI research to historical preservation, programs funded by grants from the National Endowment for the Humanities reach every corner of the U.S. Now the government has terminated those grants.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair, MPR News

item.title: White House Christmas ornament honors 150 years of culinary diplomacy practiced at state dinners
item.description: This year's official White House Christmas ornament honors more than 150 years of culinary diplomacy practiced at state dinners.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump targets ‘Anonymous’ author and former top cybersecurity official in escalation of retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump intensified his efforts to punish his critics by signing a pair of memoranda directing the Justice Department to investigate two officials from his first administration and stripping them of any security clearances they may have.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Navarro approach rubs GOP the wrong way amid trade war
item.description: Peter Navarro’s bullish personality is rubbing some Trump allies the wrong way as he takes center stage in the battle over tariffs and butts heads with other top officials in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch budget day in the House: Texas lawmakers take up $337 billion spending plan
item.description: Among hundreds of amendments are ones that focus on school vouchers, the attorney general’s office and the lottery commission.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer And Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Johnson vows to try again after GOP holdouts block action on Trump’s budget bill
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate Majority Leader John Thune are assuring Republicans they're on track seeking as much as $1.5 trillion in cuts.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republican-led House passes bill to limit nationwide orders from federal district judges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed legislation Wednesday mostly along party lines that limits the authority of federal district judges to issue nationwide orders, as Republicans react to several court rulings against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has a timeline in mind for Iran nuke deal, taps Israel to lead any potential military action
item.description: Trump told reporters Wednesday that if Iran does not give up its nuclear weapons program, military action led by Israel will be a real possibility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's 'buy' tip on social media before his tariffs pause made money for investors who listened
item.description: President Donald Trump offered financial advice on Wednesday morning that ended up making a lot of money for any lucky investor who listened to him.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — U.S. Space Command said Wednesday that it has finalized options for President Donald Trump's “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Israel would be 'leader' of Iran strike if Tehran doesn't give up nuclear weapons program
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Israel would be the “leader” of a potential military strike against Iran if Tehran doesn’t give up its nuclear weapons program. Trump made the comments ahead of this weekend's scheduled talks involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans express relief after Trump pauses tariff plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As news that President Donald Trump was backing down on most of his tariffs reached a luncheon of Senate Republicans Wednesday, the room reacted with relief, cheers and smiles.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judges in New York and Texas block Trump deportations after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: The Trump admin’s use of a wartime immigration law faces new hurdles as judges in two states block deportations, citing due process concerns and legal ambiguity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: FEMA to revoke $188M in New York City migrant grants
item.description: The money is part of the Shelter and Services Program, which helps local governments provide for migrants.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump should focus on ‘targeted tariffs’: Rep. James Comer
item.description: Comer also emphasized the importance of considering what must be produced in the United States from a national security perspective.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump move forward with firing thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court's stay, which allows the administration to execute the firings for now while it litigates in federal court, does not mean the terminations were lawful.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Christina Gatti, MPR News

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts has agreed to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Champion steps down as ethics committee chair amid conflict-of-interest questions
item.description: Minnesota Senate President Bobby Joe Champion will step down as head of the Subcommittee on Ethics as he faces questions about a possible conflict of interest related to his legal work and funding bills he has sponsored.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday, as the outbreak continues to swell.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
